subject: [PATCH v4] firmware_loader: add search= module option for multi-path firmware lookup

reviews.author: Greg Kroah-Hartman
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Greg Kroah-Hartman expressed concern about the naming of the new module option, suggesting that it should be either 'search_path' or 'path_search', as he finds the current name unclear.

Reviewer suggested moving firmware path parsing to startup or when an option is written, reducing the need for repeated cleanup logic in search paths
reviews.raw_body:
"search_path"?

Or "path_search"?

Naming is hard :(

---

Why don't we just do all the "parsing" and stripping at startup (and
when a new option is written), so we don't have to do it each and every
time we search for a firmware image?  That would put all of the
"cleanup" logic in one place and reduce the churn a bit.

Yes, it would require another local variable, but that should be fine.

thanks,

greg k-h

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author agreed to address a concern about the firmware_class.search= module option's interaction with the original path= string, promising to make changes in a future version.
reviews.raw_body:
Ok, I'll see what I can do.

Thanks,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-03-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a new module option called 'search=' to the firmware loader, which allows users to specify multiple paths for firmware lookup. The search path is tried after the original path and before hardcoded default paths. The search path can be specified as a colon-separated list of directories, with backslashes used as escape characters to embed literal colons or backslashes in the path.
